summary: Full backend testing of the Leave Management System (GrowItUp ERP). Wrote 32 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_leave_mgmt.py covering working-days calc, balance access control, validations, admin review flow, employee cancel flow, credit-monthly idempotency, reset-yearly, and an end-to-end round-trip (eligible employee -> credit -> apply -> approve -> cancel -> balance restored). All 32/32 tests passed.

minor.issue: update_employee upserts leave_balance with upsert=False (line ~931-936). If an employee has no leave_balance document yet (legacy/seeded emp), toggling paid_leave_eligible=true via PUT silently has no effect until the balance row is later created by get_or_create_leave_balance (which hard-codes paid_leave_eligible=False). In this test environment a balance row already existed for the seeded employees so this passes, but on a fresh employee who never had GET /api/leave/balance called before the PUT, eligibility would be lost. Recommend changing to upsert=True and seeding balance_id/paid_leave_balance on insert.

When approving a request, the balance is deducted using existing['paid_days'] computed at submission time. If the admin-eligible flag or balance changed between submit and approve, the split may be stale. Acceptable per spec but worth noting in docs.
Uses datetime.now() (server-local tz) to compare against body.from_date parsed as naive UTC string. Works here but can reject/accept a same-day request incorrectly across timezones. Minor.
For each request the endpoint does an N+1 lookup against employees and leave_balance. Fine for small datasets; consider $lookup or bulk fetch once volume grows.

Only resets employees whose paid_leave_balance > 0. Employees with 0 balance keep their last_credited_month, which is fine; however if the function is intended to be called at new year to clear last_credited_month universally so January credit can run for all eligible employees, this should reset last_credited_month for all balances (not just non-zero).

action_items: Consider changing leave_balance update in PUT /api/employees/{id} to upsert=True (and include balance_id/paid_leave_balance defaults) so that toggling paid_leave_eligible on a legacy/seeded employee without a pre-existing balance row is persisted.

Clarify reset-yearly semantics: should last_credited_month be reset for all employees (including zero-balance) so that January credit fires on first monthly run? Current implementation only touches non-zero balances.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 3440 lines — strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth, employees, leave, attendance, shifts, performance).
Leave endpoints duplicate the admin-check block 6+ times; extract a get_is_admin(request) dependency.
auto_credit_monthly_leave uses 'last_credited_month' = YYYY-MM-01 string; safer to store a proper date or (year, month) tuple for deterministic idempotency across timezones.

calc_leave_deduction returns float math on paid_days — rounding at 2 decimals is only applied in one branch; half-day (0.5) combined with partial paid balances could yield floats like 0.3 without rounding. Consider uniform round(..., 2).
PUT /leave/requests/{id}/review ignores body.admin_notes when None (stores empty string instead of None). Minor consistency.
test_credentials: Admin: info.growitup@gmail.com via POST /api/auth/google. Employees: john.doe@growitup.com (GM001), jane.smith@growitup.com (GM002).
seed_data_creation: Did not reseed — used existing 2 seeded employees. Test created & deleted one TEST_ employee. The E2E flow set paid_leave_eligible=true on GM001 (john.doe) and performed an approve+cancel round-trip; ending state: balance restored to pre-test amount (1 credited for May 2026), paid_leave_eligible=True remains, plus 2 audit txns (Debit + Credit) added to leave_transactions for GM001.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_leave_mgmt.py is the canonical regression suite for Leave Management. Run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/test_leave_mgmt.py -v. It auto-uses the first 2 seeded employees and cleans up its leave requests. E2E flow leaves GM001 with paid_leave_eligible=True and two audit transactions — harmless but noted.
rca of the issue: No production issue found. All 32 tests pass. The only latent bug identified by code review is the upsert=False in PUT /api/employees/{id} for the leave_balance flag toggle — reproduction would require a legacy employee with no leave_balance document; seeded employees already have one (created lazily by earlier /leave/balance calls) so test did not hit this path.
